commit_scope: Add a public read-only remote MCP endpoint that does not require OAuth credentials.

commands: cd api && python3 -m pytest tests/test_mcp_remote_no_oauth.py tests/test_agent_control_plane.py -q

cd api && python3 -m ruff check app/main.py app/routers/mcp_remote.py app/services/maintainability_audit_service.py app/services/agent_service_pipeline_status.py tests/test_mcp_remote_no_oauth.py tests/test_agent_control_plane.py

cd api && python3 -m py_compile app/main.py app/routers/mcp_remote.py app/services/maintainability_audit_service.py app/services/agent_service_pipeline_status.py tests/test_mcp_remote_no_oauth.py tests/test_agent_control_plane.py
cd web && npm ci --allow-git=none && npm run build
local_validation.summary: Prompt gate passed; targeted no-OAuth MCP tests, changed-file lint, and compile checks passed. The worktree has no api/.venv, so validation used the available worktree python3 toolchain.

ci_validation.details: Pending push, PR creation, and GitHub checks.

deploy_validation.details: No deploy requested; public endpoint validation is pending merge/deploy.

phase_gate.reason: Local proof is complete; PR and CI proof remain pending.

evidence_refs: GET /mcp and GET /api/mcp declare auth_required=false and auth_schemes=['none'].
POST /mcp and POST /api/mcp initialize without a WWW-Authenticate challenge.
The remote MCP surface exposes search/fetch and read-only registry tools while omitting mutating tools such as publish_idea and create_spec.
Focused tests assert connector-safe no-auth behavior and read-only tool exposure.
Maintainability guard no longer counts Tailwind placeholder-* CSS utility classes as fake runtime data markers.
Pipeline status follow-through checks cache and bound live GitHub collection so local runtime health checks do not fail on external gh latency.
The home page defines locale state before passing lang into LiveBreathPanel so the web build succeeds.
The home page exposes the required primary path links for /resonance, /pipeline, /nodes, and /contribute.
The home page restores the agent invitation doorway copy and canonical /come-in, /with-us, and /contribute links required by the full API suite.
The assets page keeps the literal Assets label in the rendered document for the local web contract.
The contributions and contributors pages keep their literal route labels in the rendered documents for the local web contract.
The duplicate /people/[slug] route was removed so the local Next dev server no longer conflicts with the existing /people/[id] dynamic route.
The npm MCP package metadata is bumped to 0.5.2 for public publication after merge.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Custom connector clients can target the hosted MCP URL without being forced through OAuth credential entry, and can discover/call public read-only tools.
test_flows: No-auth metadata probe for both MCP paths
MCP initialize JSON-RPC handshake without OAuth challenge
